statement.markdown: Implement a function to calculate the nth Fibonacci number. The function should use memoization to optimize the calculation. However, the current implementation contains a bug.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an integer n, representing the position of the Fibonacci number to be calculated.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the nth Fibonacci number.
statement.notes: The function should be able to handle large inputs efficiently.

hints.text: Check the memoization table for correct initialization and usage.

editorial.title: Editorial: Correct Fibonacci Memoization
editorial.intuition: The Fibonacci sequence can be efficiently calculated using memoization to store previously computed values.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve calculating each Fibonacci number from scratch, resulting in exponential time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using memoization to store the Fibonacci numbers as they are calculated.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves initializing the memoization table with base cases (F(0) = 0, F(1) = 1) and then iteratively calculating subsequent Fibonacci numbers using the stored values.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by ensuring that the memoization table is correctly initialized and updated.
editorial.dryRun: A step-by-step dry run of the solution can help identify any errors or inconsistencies in the code.
